signals.reasoning: BTC is strong and trend-supported, but a live Fed-policy veto is enough to block chasing a fresh breakout here.
ETH is participating in the same strong recovery tape, but the event brake outweighs a new long entry at these levels.
SOL has fully recovered and is trend-supported again, but it is already extended and not clean enough to chase under a live veto.
TST is far too extended and crowded to trust with a new signal while the event brake is active.
BOME is another stretched rebound name and does not clear the bar for a fresh trade under a veto board.
KNEIRO has incomplete market data in the request snapshot, so it should stay off-limits.
VVV is recovering, but the structure still looks more like noisy rebound than a clean fresh setup.
ZEREBRO still reads more like short-covering than a high-quality new entry.
WCT has bounce momentum, but the move is already far enough along that the veto should keep it watch-only.
MERL is trend-supported and strong, but it is already stretched and too event-sensitive to upgrade here.
AAVE has strong recovery structure, but the live macro-policy veto blocks a fresh breakout chase.
0G still has squeeze fuel after a liquidation flush, but the setup is too unstable to trust during a live veto.
TURBO has real short-squeeze characteristics, but it remains too thin and too jumpy for a veto board.
BRETT is one of the better raw squeeze names on the sheet, but the event brake still outweighs the setup.
XPL is constructive, but not differentiated enough to justify upgrading through a live veto.
CAKE has recovered well, but it is already too far along to call a fresh trade under headline risk.
CHILLGUY still looks more like a short-covering rebound than a clean durable trend entry.
STABLE is the strongest raw squeeze-long on the board, but the live Fed-shock veto is enough to keep it watch-only instead of actionable.
HMSTR remains too low-quality and too illiquid structurally to justify a new signal.
ORDI is constructive and has some squeeze support, but the rebound is already extended and not worth forcing under a veto.
